decisions.what: Rewrote .gitignore with sectioned rules (OS, editor, temp, dependency, build, env, coverage, package manager logs, misc) and preserved the pre-existing arvad_task_files/ ignore entry.
decisions.why: Specification 001 asked for a fresh .gitignore covering OS/editor/temp artifacts with commented sections; the existing Python-centric ignore did not match the static-HTML tech stack for this project.

decisions.what: Replaced the stub README with a full waitlist landing page README containing Features, Deployment (GitHub Pages), Customization, Form Configuration (Formspree), Analytics Setup (GA4 + Plausible), and MIT License sections.
decisions.why: Specification 002 explicitly required each of these sections with code blocks, so the placeholder single-line README was expanded to meet the documented structure.

decisions.what: Created favicon.svg as a 32x32 SVG envelope glyph on a rounded-square background using a diagonal indigo-to-purple linear gradient (#6366f1 → #a855f7).
decisions.why: Specification 003 called for a minimal, recognizable geometric shape (envelope) rendered with a purple/indigo gradient and a viewBox of 0 0 32 32; the envelope was chosen because it maps directly to the waitlist/email-capture concept.

decisions.what: Generated a real binary favicon.ico containing both 16x16 and 32x32 32bpp BMP images sharing a solid indigo color, written via a Python script that constructs valid ICONDIR / ICONDIRENTRY / BITMAPINFOHEADER structures.
decisions.why: Specification 004 required a minimal valid ICO file structure with 16x16 and 32x32 sizes; producing a spec-compliant binary avoids browser 404s while remaining a lightweight placeholder that can be replaced with a rendered version of favicon.svg later.
